assessment_items.question_content:
**Lucas Cranach created this image in consultation with [[☃ dropdown 1]]. The image outlines salvation in the Protestant tradition.**

[[☃ image 1]]
hints:
**Lucas Cranach created this image in consultation with Martin Luther.**

**[Read this essay to review](/humanities/ap-art-history/early-europe-and-colonial-americas/reformation-counter-reformation/a/cranach-law-and-gospel-law-and-grace)**

assessment_items.question_content:
**Protestants were concerned about corruption in . . .**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
**Protestants were concerned with corruption in the Catholic Church.** 

**[Read this essay to review](/humanities/art-history/art-history-1500-1600-end-of-the-renaissance-and-the-reformation/northern-renaissance/a/lucas-cranach-law-and-gospel-law-and-grace)**

assessment_items.question_content:
**The basic message of Cranach's *Law and Gospel* is that heaven is reached through [[☃ dropdown 1]] and [[☃ dropdown 2]].**


![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/b00d1de756961e505839c98af35b0d11.png)
hints:
**The message of Cranach's *Law and Gospel* is that heaven is reached through faith and God's grace.**

**[Read this essay to review](/humanities/art-history/art-history-1500-1600-end-of-the-renaissance-and-the-reformation/northern-renaissance/a/lucas-cranach-law-and-gospel-law-and-grace)**

assessment_items.question_content: ***The Law and Gospel* is concerned with the two roles that God plays: [[☃ dropdown 1]] and [[☃ dropdown 2]].**
hints:
***The Law and the Gospel* is concerned with two roles that God plays, to judge and to show mercy. On the one hand, God judges and condemns human sin; but on the other hand, God also shows mercy and forgiveness, granting unearned salvation to sinful believers.**

**[Read this essay to review](https://www.khanacademy.org/humanities/ap-art-history/early-europe-and-colonial-americas/reformation-counter-reformation/a/cranach-law-and-gospel-law-and-grace)**
